text: Fill in the blanks about the general features, joints, and surface marking of the upper limb.
questions: The upper limb has two layers of fascia: *superficial/superficial fascia* fascia (containing fat and cutaneous nerves) and *deep/deep fascia* fascia (wrapping around muscles like a tight sleeve).

The arm is divided into anterior and posterior compartments by the medial and lateral *intermuscular septa/intermuscular septum:tough connective tissue projections*. The anterior compartment contains *flexor/flexors* muscles while the posterior contains the *triceps brachii/triceps*.

Dangerous swelling within a closed fascial compartment is called *compartment syndrome/compartment syndrome:surgical emergency*, which compresses nerves and blood vessels and requires emergency treatment.

The two major superficial veins of the upper limb are the *cephalic/cephalic vein* vein (lateral side) and the *basilic/basilic vein* vein (medial side), connected at the cubital fossa by the *median cubital/median cubital vein* vein.
